news.summary: On June 27, 2025, Titan Pharmaceuticals, Inc. disclosed that it finalized a private placement deal with Blue Harbour Asset Management L.L.C-FZ, as per the securities purchase agreement. The agreement saw Blue Harbour acquire 60,000 shares of Titan's Series C Convertible Preferred Stock, which were priced at $3.40 per share. The total purchase amount was $600,000. Titan Pharmaceuticals, Inc., known as TTNP on the NASDAQ exchange, made the announcement about the completion of this transaction.
news.headline: Titan Pharmaceuticals completed a $600,000 private placement with Blue Harbour for 60,000 shares at $3.40 conversion price.

news.summary: Beijing, China, June 27, 2025 - Julong Holding Limited, a company focused on intelligent integrated solutions, has successfully completed its initial public offering (IPO) on Nasdaq (Nasdaq: JLHL). The Offering consisted of 1,250,000 Class A ordinary shares priced at US$4.00 per share. Trading of the Company's Class A ordinary shares commenced on the Nasdaq Capital Market on June 26, 2025, under the ticker symbol “JLHL.”
news.headline: Julong Holding Limited closes IPO with 1,250,000 shares at $4.00 per share on Nasdaq under ticker "JLHL" on June 26, 2025.

news.summary: On June 27, 2025, Portman Ridge Finance Corporation (NASDAQ: PTMN) shared that they received shareholder consent for the issuance of PTMN common stock related to the merger with Logan Ridge Finance Corporation (NASDAQ: LRFC). This approval came after the special meeting of shareholders on the same day. The merging entities are PTMN and LRFC, with PTMN being the acquiring company. PTMN proposed the Share Issuance Proposal, receiving a green light from the shareholders during the adjourned meeting. This decision marks a significant step towards the merger between PTMN and LRFC.
news.headline: Portman Ridge Finance Corp. gains shareholder approval for merger with Logan Ridge Finance Corp. (LRFC) on June 27, 2025.

news.summary: SiTime Corporation, a Precision Timing company based in Santa Clara, California, successfully completed a follow-on public offering on June 27, 2025. This offering included 2,012,500 shares of its common stock, with the underwriters opting to purchase additional shares in full. The shares were priced at $200.00 each for the public. After accounting for underwriting discounts, commissions, and estimated offering expenses, SiTime received approximately $387.4 million in net proceeds.
news.headline: SiTime completes public offering of 2,012,500 shares at $200/share, raising approximately $387.4 million.

news.summary: On June 27, 2025, ARRAY Technologies, Inc. announced the completion of a private offering of $345 million in convertible senior notes due July 2031. This offering includes the initial $300 million amount and the full exercise of the $45 million option to purchase additional notes. The notes, carrying a 2.875% interest rate, were specifically offered to qualified institutional buyers under Rule 144A of the Securities Act of 1933. ARRAY Technologies, Inc. is traded on the NASDAQ under the ticker symbol ARRY.
news.headline: Array Technologies closed an offering of $345m 2.875% convertible senior notes due 2031, upsized from $300m.

news.summary: On June 27, 2025, Xenon Pharmaceuticals Inc. (Nasdaq: XENE), based in Vancouver, British Columbia and Boston, was included in the Russell 3000® and Russell 2000® Indexes during their annual reconstitution. This move became effective at the end of trading on the same day. Xenon Pharmaceuticals Inc. is a biopharmaceutical company focused on neuroscience, committed to discovering, developing, and providing impactful therapeutics for patients. The Russell 3000® Index measures the performance of the largest 3000 U.S. companies, while the Russell 2000® Index tracks the 2000 smaller-cap companies.
news.headline: Xenon Pharmaceuticals Inc. (Nasdaq: XENE) added to Russell 3000® and Russell 2000® Indexes on June 27, 2025.

news.summary: JA Mining, a UK-based cloud mining provider, unveiled new AI-driven mining services to enhance efficiency and sustainability in cryptocurrency mining. The company's automated system intelligently selects cryptocurrencies and mining strategies, adapting to market and computing changes. The system was launched as Bitcoin reached $108,000. JA Mining aims to offer a user-friendly way for clients to earn passive income from digital assets without managing physical mining equipment. The company is known for using renewable energy sources globally. This development underscores JA Mining's commitment to technological advancements and sustainability in the digital asset space.
news.headline: JA Mining launches AI-powered cloud mining solution for efficient crypto mining. Expert system adjusts to market changes like when Bitcoin hit $108,000.

news.summary: Recently, it was reported that thousands of U.S. crypto investors have been issued warning letters by the Internal Revenue Service (IRS), as part of a growing effort to ensure tax compliance in the digital assets space. CoinLedger CEO David Kemmerer disclosed in an interview that the number of IRS letters sent to crypto investors has surged by 758% in the past two months, based on internal data from CoinLedger. This increase precedes the implementation of new IRS reporting rules, known as 1099-DA regulations, scheduled to begin in January 2026. These rules will mandate all crypto brokers to report the gross proceeds and cost basis of digital asset sales, improving the IRS's oversight on investor gains and losses. The warning letters are a part of the IRS's strategy to strengthen tax enforcement in the crypto sector.
news.headline: IRS sending warning letters to U.S. crypto investors has surged 758% in last two months, reaching thousands as agency ramps up tax compliance efforts amidst new reporting rules coming in January 2026.

news.summary: Stocks on Wall Street saw a significant turnaround on Friday afternoon following President Donald Trump's surprise decision to halt all trade talks with Canada. Trump's announcement, shared on Truth Social, cited Canada's implementation of a Digital Services Tax on American tech companies as the reason for the abrupt change in stance. This move, deemed a direct assault on the US by Trump, swiftly led to the termination of trade negotiations. Within the next seven days, Canada will be informed of the tariff they will face when conducting business with the US. The sudden shift in trade policy led to a downturn in investor sentiment, prompting them to flee from risk assets. The complete story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Trump abruptly ends trade negotiations with Canada over digital services tax, causing Wall Street turmoil. Market reverses course, investors flee risk assets. Trump directs a tariffs message to Canada within seven days.

news.summary: The cryptocurrency markets showed minimal movement on Friday due to low trading volumes and signs of temporary technical exhaustion. The prices of popular cryptocurrencies were as follows: Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) at $106,654.83, Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH) at $2,400.50, Solana (CRYPTO: SOL) at $141.12, XRP (CRYPTO: XRP) at $2.09, Dogecoin (CRYPTO: DOGE) at $0.1588, and Shiba Inu (CRYPTO: SHIB) at $0.00001112. Noteworthy statistics include a 7.2% decrease in Bitcoin's large transaction volume and a 2.5% increase in daily active addresses. Transactions exceeding $100,000 dropped from 11,556 to 11,383 in a single day, and exchanges netflows decreased by 41.7%. As reported by Coinglass, 70,686 traders were liquidated in the past 24 hours, resulting in losses of $263.27 million. In recent developments, Michael Saylor suggests that Bitcoin treasury companies have the potential for further growth, while discussions arise on the risks and benefits of the corporate Bitcoin buying trend. Additionally, there is growing interest in investment options offering yields of up to 8% in Bitcoin. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Cryptocurrency trade largely flat, Bitcoin at $106,654.83, Ethereum at $2,400.50, XRP at $2.09, Dogecoin at $0.1588. Bitcoin volume down 7.2%, 70,686 traders liquidated for $263.27 million in 24 hours. Various notable developments discussed.

news.summary: In the latest update, it has been reported that the number of confirmed COVID-19 cases has risen to over 5.4 million globally, with the death toll surpassing 345,000. The United States leads in both cases and fatalities, with over 1.6 million cases and more than 98,000 deaths. Brazil follows closely behind with over 330,000 cases and nearly 22,000 deaths. In Europe, the United Kingdom has the highest death toll with over 36,000 fatalities. The pandemic has affected countries worldwide, with efforts focused on combating the spread and minimizing the impact on public health and economies.
news.headline: Producer Bruckheimer sets aside egos to release 'F1' in cinemas.

news.summary: Nick van Eck, who is the co-founder and CEO of Agora, the company responsible for the stablecoin Anchorage, announced that they plan to discontinue the stablecoin. Van Eck cited inaccuracies and a lack of consistency in the framework being used as reasons for this decision.
news.headline: Anchorage 'Safety Matrix' sparks controversy with delisting of stablecoins. Agora CEO disputes decision, citing inconsistencies.

news.summary: In response to the recent surge in COVID-19 cases, the government has announced new measures to combat the spread of the virus. As of today, there have been a total of 10,000 new cases reported, bringing the total number of confirmed cases to 150,000. The new restrictions include a mandatory mask mandate in all public indoor spaces and a limit on gatherings to no more than 50 people. Additionally, all schools will transition to online learning for the next three weeks. These measures are set to be in place for at least a month, with the possibility of extension depending on the trajectory of the virus. The government is urging the public to adhere to these guidelines to help curb the spread of COVID-19.
news.headline: On 6/27, price predictions for 10 cryptocurrencies: BTC, ETH, XRP, BNB, SOL, DOGE, ADA, HYPE, BCH, LINK.

news.summary: A recent report indicates that the global economy is projected to grow by 5.6% in 2021, which is a significant increase from the 3.5% contraction seen in 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic. The recovery is primarily driven by strong growth in advanced economies, with the United States expected to grow by 6.4% and China by 8.4%. However, emerging market and developing economies are facing slower recovery rates, with an estimated growth of 5.3%. Despite the overall positive outlook, uncertainties remain due to ongoing challenges such as vaccine distribution, new COVID-19 variants, and social unrest in some regions. Policymakers are urged to continue providing support to ensure a sustainable and inclusive recovery.
news.headline: Jerry Bruckheimer discusses creating F1 in Hollywood on YouTube.

news.summary: The recent report reveals that global carbon dioxide emissions reached a record high of 36.4 billion metric tons in 2021. This marks a significant increase from the previous year and raises concerns about the acceleration of climate change. The data also shows that China was the largest emitter, accounting for 28% of the total global emissions, followed by the United States with 14%. The rise in emissions is attributed to the rebound in economic activities following the COVID-19 pandemic and the continued reliance on fossil fuels. Experts warn that urgent action is needed to curb emissions and mitigate the impact of climate change.

news.summary: Local families in Northwest Arkansas are receiving crucial assistance during the summer through a Community Resource Rally held in Rogers. As school cafeterias are shut for the season, many families are struggling to provide meals. The rally, which took place on June 27, 2025, aimed to address this issue by offering essential aid to those in need. This initiative is crucial as it helps ensure that families have access to food during the summer months, with a significant number of families benefitting from the support provided.
news.headline: StarKist, Feed the Children, and Feed 479 collaborate to help 400 families in NW Arkansas with food resources.

news.summary: An affordable approach is being developed to decrease dependence on costly diagnostic tests like MRI, CT scans, PET scans, and echocardiograms. This cost-efficient methodology is geared towards making healthcare more accessible and reducing the financial burden on patients and the healthcare system. By implementing this strategy, significant savings can be achieved by avoiding high expenses associated with these advanced diagnostic procedures. This initiative seeks to make healthcare services more economical and efficient, providing a sustainable solution to the rising healthcare costs.
news.headline: RetinalGenix partners with LabCorp for DNA/GPS screening to reduce reliance on costly diagnostic procedures like MRI, CT, PET scans, and echocardiograms.
